user_query:
Hi! I’m getting ready for our Barrhaven United Church tech volunteers huddle and I want to help everyone by bringing a clear snapshot of the computer we’ll use for slides and livestream. Please do a local system status inspection guided by the thresholds in input/config/checks.yaml, and produce three deliverables plus a validation log:

1) Collect status and save a structured report:
- Measure these on this machine:
  - disk_used_percent and disk_free_percent for the filesystem that contains the current workspace
  - mem_total_mib, mem_available_mib, and mem_free_percent
  - cpu_cores
  - python_version (string from the default "python" executable)
  - required_tools_present: map of each tool in config required_tools to true/false based on PATH
- Compute booleans using config thresholds:
  - disk_ok = disk_free_percent >= disk_free_percent_min
  - mem_ok = mem_free_percent >= mem_free_percent_min
  - python_ok = python_version >= python_min_version (semantic version comparison)
  - tools_ok = all required_tools are present
  - overall_ok = all of the above are true
- Save the full report to outputs/report/system_status.json with keys: timestamp, workspace_path, disk_used_percent, disk_free_percent, mem_total_mib, mem_available_mib, mem_free_percent, cpu_cores, python_version, required_tools_present (object), disk_ok, mem_ok, python_ok, tools_ok, overall_ok, errors (array of strings if any checks fail to run).
- Also capture a raw log of the commands you ran and their outputs (or Python snippets you executed and their printed results) to outputs/raw/commands_and_outputs.log.

2) Prepare meeting notes with action items:
- Using input/agenda/meeting_agenda.md as a base, generate outputs/notes/tech_meeting_notes.md that keeps the template sections but fills in:
  - “System Status Summary”: include a brief bullet list of each check with PASS/FAIL and the measured values versus thresholds from the JSON.
  - “Action Items”: bullet points for each failing check, written as concrete tasks (e.g., “Free at least 10% disk space on workspace drive to reach 20% free.”, “Install Git and add to PATH.”). If overall_ok is true, include a single action item to “Confirm projector hookup and rehearsal flow.”

3) Rewrite the volunteer email for tone and brevity:
- Read input/messages/rough_email.txt and rewrite it into a friendly, clear, and concise note (≤ 180 words) that summarizes the current system status at a high level (OK or issues to address), invites help, and confirms the meeting time mentioned in the draft. Save to outputs/messages/volunteer_email_rewrite.txt. Keep the intent and details (date/time and requests) but improve the tone (warm, inclusive) and clarity.

4) Run a simple validation and record it:
- Run a local validation command or script that re-reads outputs/report/system_status.json and checks that disk_ok, mem_ok, python_ok, and tools_ok align with the measured values and config thresholds. Save the console output (with a PASS/FAIL line per check) to outputs/tests/validation.txt.

Notes and constraints:
- Use only local inspection (no internet). If a command isn’t available, record a clear error in the errors array and proceed with the rest.
- All output paths must match exactly as specified.
- The JSON fields must be present even if some values are unavailable; use null and add an explanatory message in errors when needed.
- Base all pass/fail decisions and action items strictly on the config in input/config/checks.yaml and the locally observed values.

Thanks for helping me make this easier for our volunteers!
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
